{{Quote|WOW! That's some scary–looking ship!|Nikki Idd|<ref name="Droids:UW" />}}
The [[Annoo-dat]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Tig Fromm]], one of the leaders of the [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] criminal organization, used a '''starship''' as [[Gender/Legends|his]] personal [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transport]]. The triangular-shaped [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was heavily armed and saw use during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Description==
The [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of the [[Annoo-dat]] [[Gangster/Legends|gangster]] [[Tig Fromm]] had a basic shape that was a slightly triangulated red and yellow [[Color/Legends|colored]] disc with yellow landing gear on the underside. The ship had one large main [[Engine/Legends|engine]] with large blue and yellow fins on either side. The section of the ship situated on top of the disc was blue and yellow in color and had heavy armaments mounted on it.<ref name="Droids:UW" />

==History==
Tig Fromm, one of the leaders of [[Fromm Gang/Legends|Fromm Gang]] crime organization, used the starship<ref name="Droids:UW" /> during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] a few [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Blog">The [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: Blogs|Blog]] [[The Droids Re-Animated|''The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1'']] noted that the ''[[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO]]'' series takes place [[15 BBY/Legends|15 years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], or a few years after the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ended in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], according to the reference book ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Fromm once attempted to improve the ship's weaponry further by infiltrating the [[Lonn Idd's repair station|repair station]] of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] named [[Lonn Idd]], who was known for his [[Laser/Legends|laser]] expertise. There Fromm forced Idd to install the scientist's new powerful laser by holding his son and daughter, [[Vik Idd|Vik]] and [[Nikki Idd|Nikki]], respectively, as hostages. After the laser was installed, [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and Vik managed to trap Fromm and his companions, who were handed over to the [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|authorities]] afterwards.<ref name="Droids:UW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tig Fromm's ship was introduced in the [[June]] [[1986]] [[Marvel Comics]] issue [[Droids (1986) 2|''Droids'' (1986) 2]], which was written by [[David Manak|Dave Manak]] and drawn by [[John Romita Sr.]] In the comic, the ship was seen docked in Lonn Idd's repair station.

==Appearances==
